claim: At modulus m, the equivalence class [k]_m = { x in R : quantize(x,m)=k } is isomorphic to a resonance tolerance bandwidth centered on integer eigenvalue k; Hensel lift m -> m*p is progressive bandwidth narrowing.

arnold_tongue.description: Two coupled phase oscillators dphi_1/dt = 1 + K*sin(phi_2 - phi_1), dphi_2/dt = (1+delta) + K*sin(phi_1 - phi_2). Predict locked-fraction (mode-mass of QA-binned phase difference) rises monotonically in K and falls monotonically in m at fixed K.

integer_roundtrip.description: For any continuous input x, quantize(x,m) -> int64 bin -> bin-center -> re-bin preserves the integer bin assignment. Asserts S2/A1 compliance of TypeA-style encoding: no float drift, no auto-reducing fractions.

hensel_reference.claim: Orbit-family count on S_{3^k} grows with k; Pudelko/Hensel prediction: mod-3=2 families, mod-9=5 families, mod-27=further splitting. Progressive bandwidth narrowing.

permissibility_filter_role.syntax_layer: QA integer equivalence class [k]_m (modular arithmetic, bin assignment)
permissibility_filter_role.semantics_layer: SVP sympathetic transmission bandwidth centered on physical resonance k (Arnold tongue width, Q-factor)
permissibility_filter_role.filter_function: bandwidth(k, m) proportional to 2*pi/m (inverse of modulus selectivity)

physics_analogs: Quantum eigenvalue selection under Hamiltonian perturbation
